LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RICHARD SPIGHT, DIABLO COMMERCIAL PROPERTIES LLC, MICHAEL SPIGHT AND TYRIS CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION: DEFENDANTS RICHARD SPIGHT, DIABLO COMMERCIAL PROPERTIES LLC, MICHAEL SPIGHT AND TYRIS CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO ALL SEVEN CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF AMERICAN TOWERS LLC IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL SEVEN CAUSES OF ACTION. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR FURTHER DETAILS.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LEWIS GILLIAN, CSR NO: 12355, EMAIL: LGILLIAN@SFTC.ORG. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cynthia Larsen, Esq. 916-329-7970 of Orick for Plaintiff.
Jill Rowe, Esq. 415-433-1900 of Cooper White & Cooper for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court admonishes parties and shares his tentative ruling of sustaining demurrer with leave to amend and to overrule on the second group of claims. Both sides argue. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court takes a brief recess to hear Ex parte Applications.

11:33 am
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS RICHARD SPIGHT, DIABLO COMMERCIAL PROPERTIES LLC, MICHAEL SPIGHT, AND TYRIS CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the first three causes of action. As to the last three causes of action, the Court grants the request of Plaintiff to amend those claims. Plaintiff has 30 days leave to amend. Defendant may file a responsive pleading.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcripts.)

Defendant's Counsel to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR# 6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Continued To Feb-07-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Cynthia J. Larsen of the law offices of Orrick Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP (916-329-7970) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jill B. Rowe of the law offices of Cooper White & Cooper (415-433-1900) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANTS RICHARD SPIGHT, DIABLO COMMERCIAL PROPERTIES LLC, MICHAEL SPIGHT and TYRIS CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to all seven causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff American Towers LLC is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to all seven causes of action. Each of the seven claims alleged by ATC necessarily requires that ATC will be able to prove that in 1997 when it entered into the Asset Purchase Agreement and subsequently ATC could reasonably believe that, when the then existing lease expired and it ceased to have the right to remain at the Site, it would under all circumstances retain ownership of the Tower. However, the unambiguous language of paragraph 13 of the lease shows that as a matter of law ATC could not reasonably believe that, when the then existing lease expired and it ceased to have the right to remain at the Site, it would under all circumstances retain ownership of the Tower. Paragraph 13 of the lease also shows that as a matter of law: a) the alleged misrepresentations forming the bases for the first two causes of action were not in fact misrepresentations; b) the alleged breach forming the basis of the third cause of action did not occur; and c) the alleged acts of interference forming the bases of the fourth through seventh causes of action were not in any way wrongful or actionable. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Lewis Gillian, CSR No: 12355, email: lgillian@sftc.org. (D302)

Attorney, Jill B. Rowe of the law offices of Cooper White & Cooper (415-433-1900) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

DEFENDANTS RICHARD SPIGHT, DIABLO COMMERCIAL PROPERTIES LLC, MICHAEL SPIGHT and TYRIS CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, as to portion of the complaint filed by plaintiff American Towers LLC is off calendar in light of the ruling on the moving defendants' demurrer to the complaint.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Lewis Gillian, CSR No: 12355, email: lgillian@sftc.org. (D302)
